name: mcp_server/docs/cursor_rules.md
applicability: Pattern for AI-client operating protocol: search-first, filter-by-entity-type, capture-immediately, follow-procedures. Maps directly onto home CLAUDE.md §4 (Behavioral Guardrails) + MEMORY.md types (user/feedback/project/reference) philosophy.
home_connection: Home CLAUDE.md already mandates 'Ask on first confusion' and 'Read SKILL.md before following skill format'. Graphiti's cursor_rules.md adds 'search-graph-memory-first' discipline — directly usable as a memory-skill operating protocol.

name: Root CLAUDE.md (graphiti repo)
applicability: Dated versioned model catalog (GPT-5 family, Claude 4.5, Gemini 2.5 as of Nov 2025). Separation of AGENTS.md (generic repo guide) vs CLAUDE.md (AI-specific).
home_connection: Home CLAUDE.md v6.0 has §1 Stack Versions for JS/framework deps but NO versioned LLM-model table. Graphiti pattern is directly transferable — keeps AI assistants from 'correcting' current model names to older training-data-familiar ones.

name: AGENTS.md (graphiti repo)
applicability: Companion doc to CLAUDE.md for generic repo guidance (structure, build, test, style). Separates AI-specific from human-onboarding concerns.
home_connection: Home has CLAUDE.md only, no AGENTS.md. Pattern useful if considering multi-agent coexistence (Copilot CLI, Codex, Gemini CLI) — each reads its own doc.

name: MCP entity types baseline
applicability: 9-type starter ontology for agent memory: Preference / Requirement / Procedure / Location / Event / Organization / Document / Topic / Object. Configurable via YAML.
home_connection: Home MEMORY.md uses 4 types (user/feedback/project/reference). Graphiti's ontology is broader and action-oriented (Preference, Procedure, Requirement) vs SoNash's document-oriented. Worth considering a hybrid or cross-walk for episodic-memory skill.

name: arXiv 2501.13956 — Zep paper
applicability: The underlying research: 'Zep: A Temporal Knowledge Graph Architecture for Agent Memory'. Foundational reading if considering temporal memory patterns.
home_connection: Home has episodic-memory skill (semantic search on conversations). Zep paper is the state-of-the-art reference for temporal knowledge-graph-based agent memory — directly adjacent technology.

name: LangGraph agent guide
applicability: Walks building an agent that uses graphiti as memory with LangGraph.
home_connection: Home does not use LangGraph. Low direct applicability, but demonstrates pattern of 'agent with external memory tool'.

name: examples/opentelemetry/
applicability: OTEL tracing setup with ConsoleSpanExporter, injected via constructor parameter, no-op default.
home_connection: Home has no unified tracing. Pattern is clean (optional tracer + prefix) — applicable to any home CLI/tool where observability is opt-in.

name: examples/langgraph-agent/
applicability: Notebook showing LangGraph + Graphiti integration.
home_connection: Not using LangGraph; low relevance. Useful only if home pivots to agent-graph orchestration.

name: examples/quickstart/
applicability: 4 backend variants (Neo4j, FalkorDB, Neptune, dense_vs_normal_ingestion). Entry-level API surface demo.
home_connection: If home were to adopt graphiti (Trial/Extract), this is the start line.

name: examples/ecommerce/
applicability: Product-graph demo with sample manybirds JSON data.
home_connection: SoNash is a recovery notebook, not e-commerce. Low relevance.

name: examples/podcast/
applicability: Long-form transcript ingestion with parser. Shows pattern for processing large text streams into episodes.
home_connection: Home has media-analysis skill that transcribes YouTube/TikTok. Graphiti's podcast example demonstrates how to feed transcript data into a graph — possible post-transcription memory sink pattern.

name: Driver Operations Redesign spec
applicability: In-repo architecture RFC for moving from data-class methods to namespace-pattern operations. 3-layer stack, non-breaking Phase 1.
home_connection: Pattern quality reference for home refactors. SoNash firestore-service.ts follows a similar repository-pattern — could crosswalk 'Operations ABC' pattern if ever decoupling drivers.

name: Telemetry design (opt-out, content-excluded)
applicability: Transparent 'what we collect / what we don't' README section; fail-silent implementation; pytest auto-disable; points at source file. PostHog-backed anon identifier.
home_connection: Home statusline has telemetry concerns (statusline collects session metadata). Graphiti's framing — explicit 'we never collect: content, API keys, paths' — is directly applicable as a transparency pattern.

name: SEMAPHORE_LIMIT tuning table
applicability: Per-provider RPM-to-concurrency mapping (OpenAI Tier 1-4, Anthropic default/high, Ollama hardware-dependent). Symptom→dial diagnosis guide.
home_connection: Home already handles 429 rate limits in Cloud Functions (CLAUDE.md §2 Security Rule 3). Graphiti's tuning-by-tier approach could extend to operational guidance for home skills that batch-call LLMs (e.g., deep-research searcher agents).

name: Cross-encoder boolean-log-probs reranking
applicability: Uses OpenAI/Gemini log-probs on a boolean classification prompt for reranking (instead of a dedicated reranker model).
home_connection: Home doesn't do reranking today; if recall/search over memory ever needs reranking, this pattern is cheaper than a dedicated reranker.

name: Structured-output-as-hard-floor
applicability: Explicit warning that LLM pluggability has a floor: structured output (OpenAI, Gemini) required for reliable operation; smaller models will fail.
home_connection: Anti-pattern / guard-rail pattern. Home deep-research agents assume any Claude model works. Graphiti's stance — 'pluggable but not equal' — is worth adopting for any home skill that uses structured JSON output.

name: .github/workflows/claude-code-review*.yml (2 workflows)
applicability: Graphiti ships with automated AND manual Claude Code review workflows in CI, alongside a general 'claude.yml'. Signal: AI-assisted dev tooling is active, tested, and in production.
home_connection: Home CC-OS mission directly overlaps. Graphiti's workflow names (claude-code-review, claude-code-review-manual, claude) suggest a pattern worth extracting: dual auto/manual invocation modes + a generic catchall.

name: unit_tests.yml — two-stage test job
applicability: Splits CI into (1) fast unit tests (DISABLE_* env vars to skip optional DB drivers) + (2) DB-integration tests with real Neo4j + FalkorDB services via health checks.
home_connection: Home has pre-commit hooks with patterns:check; could adopt similar two-stage split if integration tests grow beyond httpsCallable mocks.

name: codeql.yml + lint.yml + typecheck.yml
applicability: Standard OSS CI triad: CodeQL static analysis + Ruff linting + Pyright type checking.
home_connection: Home already has sonarcloud for static analysis. Minor reference value.

name: mcp_server config.yaml with env-var expansion
applicability: Hierarchical YAML config with ${VAR:default} expansion; 3-tier override (config file > env var > CLI arg); separate configs per deployment target (falkordb-combined, falkordb-separate, neo4j).
home_connection: Home uses env vars + settings.json, no hierarchical YAML. Pattern applicable if a future home tool needs deployment variants.

name: CONTRIBUTING.md RFC-before-PR
applicability: New drivers/LLM clients/embedders require a GitHub issue RFC before PR. Bug fixes prioritized.
home_connection: Home is solo-developer; low applicability. Noted as a scaling pattern if ever inviting external contributors.

name: Dockerfile non-root + uv package manager
applicability: Non-root user, uv for dependency install, multi-stage build pattern.
home_connection: Home does not ship Docker images. Low direct applicability.

name: help.getzep.com/graphiti
applicability: Hosted guides and API reference (likely Mintlify-style). Not replicated in-repo.
home_connection: Reference material if seriously evaluating Graphiti as a dependency. Skipped fetch per CLAUDE.md (docs lookups via context7).

name: Zep vs Graphiti framing
applicability: Explicit OSS/SaaS comparison table. Sets user expectations: Graphiti = engine, Zep = managed platform with dashboard/SDKs/SLA.
home_connection: Pattern useful if SoNash ever ships OSS components alongside a hosted service. Transparency about what's open vs managed prevents rug-pull concerns.
